Musical artist

**Sangeeta Shankar** ([Marathi](/wiki/Marathi_language "Marathi language"): संगीता शंकर; born 12 August 1965) is an Indian classical [violinist](/wiki/Violin "Violin") who performs [Hindustani classical music](/wiki/Hindustani_classical_music "Hindustani classical music") and [fusion](/wiki/Fusion_music "Fusion music"). She is the daughter and protégé of awarded violinist [N. Rajam](/wiki/N._Rajam "N. Rajam").

She has performed internationally and follows the g*ayaki ang* technique of reproducing the human voice on the violin. She passed on the tradition to her daughters [Ragini](/wiki/Ragini_Shankar "Ragini Shankar") and [Nandini Shankar](/wiki/Nandini_Shankar "Nandini Shankar"). Their family includes her husband Shankar Devraj and son-in-law [Mahesh Raghvan](/wiki/Mahesh_Raghvan "Mahesh Raghvan").

## Performing career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Sangeeta_Shankar&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Performing career")]

Shankar began accompanying her mother at the age of 13 and made her debut solo performance when she was 16. She plays the *gayaki ang*, a style often referred to as the 'singing violin', which aims to convey the emotions of a human voice through the violin.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Entrepreneurship and other projects

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Sangeeta_Shankar&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Entrepreneurship and other projects")]

In 1999, Shankar worked on the TV series *Swar Sadhana*, a program that aimed to educate the masses about Indian music. This series was created to increase awareness and reach of Indian classical music. *Swar Sadhana* utilized an experimental story format and featured various prominent celebrities including [Madhuri Dixit](/wiki/Madhuri_Dixit "Madhuri Dixit"), [Javed Akhtar](/wiki/Javed_Akhtar "Javed Akhtar"), [Zakir Hussain](/wiki/Zakir_Hussain_(musician) "Zakir Hussain (musician)"), [Birju Maharaj](/wiki/Birju_Maharaj "Birju Maharaj"), [Jagjit Singh](/wiki/Jagjit_Singh "Jagjit Singh"), [Naushad](/wiki/Naushad "Naushad"), [Amol Palekar](/wiki/Amol_Palekar "Amol Palekar"), [Pankaj Udhas](/wiki/Pankaj_Udhas "Pankaj Udhas"), [Yukta Mookhey](/wiki/Yukta_Mookhey "Yukta Mookhey"), [Kanak Rele](/wiki/Kanak_Rele "Kanak Rele"), [Suresh Wadkar](/wiki/Suresh_Wadkar "Suresh Wadkar"), [N. Rajam](/wiki/N._Rajam "N. Rajam"), [Sadhana Sargam](/wiki/Sadhana_Sargam "Sadhana Sargam"), [Shankar Mahadevan](/wiki/Shankar_Mahadevan "Shankar Mahadevan"), [Annu Kapoor](/wiki/Annu_Kapoor "Annu Kapoor"), [Veena Sahasrabuddhe](/wiki/Veena_Sahasrabuddhe "Veena Sahasrabuddhe"), among others.[[2]](#cite_note-2) It is currently available on [YouTube](/wiki/YouTube "YouTube") as "Knowledge Series".[[3]](#cite_note-3) Twenty years later, between 2020 and 2021, Shankar would work on another educational series that would be available on YouTube. Titled *22 Shrutis Simplified*, it aimed to help learners understand and apply the knowledge of the 22 [shrutis](/wiki/Shruti_(music) "Shruti (music)").[[4]](#cite_note-4)

In 2015, Shankar founded School of Music in Whistling Woods International with [Subhash Ghai](/wiki/Subhash_Ghai "Subhash Ghai"), which she led until 2021. She remains a member of the academic advisory board of the institution.[[5]](#cite_note-5) Since 2014, she has also taught violin at the Rajam School of Violin, a virtual school for learning *gayaki Ang* from the family of N. Rajam.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Since 2022, Shankar has been providing educational courses for media professionals and music students on music production.[[6]](#cite_note-6) She is the founder and director of a music company, Legendary Legacy Promotions Pvt. Ltd., which consists of a storehouse of classical music and other products.

## Awards

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Sangeeta_Shankar&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Awards")]

Sangeeta Shankar received the [Sangeet Natak Academi Award](/wiki/Sangeet_Natak_Akademi_Award "Sangeet Natak Akademi Award") in 2021 from the [President of India](/wiki/Ram_Nath_Kovind "Ram Nath Kovind") for her work in music education.[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8) Her workshops, seminars, and educational videos have opened Indian classical music and culture to different sectors – the masses, children, young music directors, media professionals, music students, and musicians.

## Discography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Sangeeta_Shankar&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Discography")]

* *[Tabula Rasā](/wiki/Tabula_Ras%C4%81 "Tabula Rasā")* (fusion) with [Vishwa Mohan Bhatt](/wiki/Vishwa_Mohan_Bhatt "Vishwa Mohan Bhatt") and [Béla Fleck](/wiki/B%C3%A9la_Fleck "Béla Fleck"), nominated for the [Grammy Award for Best World Music Album](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Best_Global_Music_Album "Grammy Award for Best Global Music Album") in 1997[[9]](#cite_note-CDUDetails-9)
* *Melody & Rhythm* with [Zakir Hussain](/wiki/Zakir_Hussain_(musician) "Zakir Hussain (musician)")[[10]](#cite_note-Sangeeta_Shankar_on_Yahoo!_Music-10)
* *Violin Dynasty* (Raag Bageshree) with [N. Rajam](/wiki/N._Rajam "N. Rajam")[[11]](#cite_note-11)
* *Aasha* (Raag Jog)[[10]](#cite_note-Sangeeta_Shankar_on_Yahoo!_Music-10)
* *Kumari Sangeeta* (Raag Bihag, Chayanat)
* *Sensitive Strains of Violin* (Raag Sohini, Bheem)[[9]](#cite_note-CDUDetails-9)
* *A Delicate Touch* (Raag Jogkauns, Desh)
* *Together* (Ragam Bhairavi, Malavi, Bilaharai, Todi) with N. Rajam[[9]](#cite_note-CDUDetails-9)
* *Dedications to Dawn* (Raag Mian ki Todi, Bairagi, Suha Sugharai)
* *Sangeeta Shankar* (Raag Todi, Bairagi)
* *Music therapy for Migraine – Times Music* (Raag Darbari Kanada)
* *Saundarya* (Raag Shyam Kalyaan)[[12]](#cite_note-12)

## References

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Sangeeta_Shankar&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: References")]

1. **[^](#cite_ref-1)** ["Gayaki Ang"](https://www.firstpost.com/living/violin-maestro-dr-n-rajam-daughter-sangeeta-shankar-granddaughters-ragini-and-nandini-on-their-three-generation-spanning-art-4439771.html).
